blocks.html: Python can save information to a file so it isn't lost when the program stops. In this browser, files live in a temporary in-memory disk that resets when you reload the page — perfect for practicing safely.

blocks.starter:
with open("myfile.txt", "w") as f:
    f.write("Hello, file!")

with open("myfile.txt", "r") as f:
    content = f.read()

print("File said:", content)
blocks.label: Run Code

blocks.html: You can write several lines, then read them back one at a time with readlines().

blocks.starter:
lines = ["Dogs are great\n", "Cats are great too\n", "So are hamsters\n"]

with open("animals.txt", "w") as f:
    f.writelines(lines)

with open("animals.txt", "r") as f:
    for line in f.readlines():
        print("Line:", line.strip())
blocks.label: Run Code

blocks.html: Write your own list of 3 favorite foods to a file called foods.txt, one per line. Then open it again and print each one back out.
